questions.q: What is the main function of the root hair cells in plants?
questions.answer: Root hair cells play a crucial role in the absorption of water and minerals from the soil. These hair-like structures increase the surface area for absorption, allowing plants to obtain essential nutrients.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the xylem tissue in plants?
questions.answer: The xylem tissue in plants is responsible for the transport of water, minerals, and sugars from the roots to the leaves and other parts of the plant. It consists of dead cells that provide structural support and facilitate the upward movement of water.

questions.q: What is the main difference between monocots and dicots in terms of their stem structure?
questions.answer: Monocots have a scaly or hairy stem, while dicots have a woody or herbaceous stem. Monocots also have a single cotyledon (seed leaf) during germination, whereas dicots have two cotyledons.
key_points: Scaly or hairy stem in monocots
Woody or herbaceous stem in dicots

questions.q: What is the function of the guard cells in stomatal apparatus?
questions.answer: Guard cells are responsible for the opening and closing of stomata, which regulate gas exchange and transpiration in plants. They are responsible for controlling the amount of CO2 entering the leaf and water vapor released into the atmosphere.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the apical meristem in plants?
questions.answer: The apical meristem is responsible for the primary growth of plants, which involves the production of new cells at the tips of roots and shoots. It allows plants to increase in length and develop new tissues.

questions.q: What is the main difference between primary and secondary growth in plants?
questions.answer: Primary growth occurs at the tips of roots and shoots, resulting in an increase in length, while secondary growth occurs in the lateral meristems, resulting in an increase in girth. This is evident in the development of tree trunks and branches.

questions.q: What is the role of the pericycle in plants?
questions.answer: The pericycle is a layer of cells surrounding the vascular cambium in plants. It gives rise to the cork cambium, which produces the bark of trees, and also contributes to the formation of secondary xylem and phloem.
key_points: Layer of cells surrounding vascular cambium
Contributes to secondary xylem and phloem

questions.q: What is the main function of the phloem tissue in plants?
questions.answer: The phloem tissue in plants is responsible for the transport of sugars, amino acids, and other organic compounds produced by photosynthesis. It also plays a role in the transport of hormones and defense signals.
key_points: Transport of sugars and amino acids
Transport of hormones and defense signals

questions.q: What is the difference between the endodermis and the pericycle in plants?
questions.answer: The endodermis is a single layer of cells surrounding the vascular tissue in plants, while the pericycle is a layer of cells surrounding the vascular cambium. The endodermis is responsible for the regulation of water and minerals, while the pericycle gives rise to the cork cambium.
key_points: Single layer of cells in endodermis
Gives rise to cork cambium in pericycle

questions.q: What is the role of the cambium in plants?
questions.answer: The cambium is a layer of cells responsible for the secondary growth of plants, producing new xylem and phloem tissues. It allows plants to increase in girth and develop new tissues, such as the bark of trees.
key_points: Layer of cells responsible for secondary growth
